page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 16 vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 2.5 GHz. The system includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, and a local 225 GB NVMe SSD. It runs on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between CPU-only tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and compression tests, falls in the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core CPU operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently score in the middle 50% tier. In contrast, the hardware accelerator delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference prompt processing on small (135M) and medium (7B) models, alongside strong performance in the top 25% for text generation on those same models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.4xlarge offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by pairing a mid-tier Turing-generation GPU with standard DDR4 memory and a moderate local NVMe drive. The main tradeoff lies in its single-core CPU performance, which is weak compared to newer compute-optimized instances. This makes it less optimal for CPU-bound single-threaded tasks, though its multi-core and GPU capabilities offset this for parallel workloads.

This instance is configured for workloads that require hardware acceleration alongside moderate local storage and network throughput. Suitable use cases include machine learning inference for small to medium models, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations that can leverage the 20 Gbps network baseline and local NVMe SSD.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 16 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. It includes a 225 GB local NVMe SSD and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU and memory performance, but weak single-core CPU performance. Conversely, the instance excels in GPU-driven tasks, achieving top-tier results in LLM inference prompt processing for small and medium models. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-effective platform for GPU workloads by balancing entry-level acceleration with standard system memory, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded CPU efficiency. It is suited for machine learning inference, image processing, ray tracing, and database operations.

tagline: An entry-level GPU instance pairing dedicated Intel Xeon processors with an NVIDIA T4 accelerator and local NVMe storage.

Features 16 vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8259CL and 64 GB DDR4 RAM
Includes 225 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
